story.iWant: convert all string-based enums to integer-based enums in the database schema and TypeScript code
story.soThat: the application has improved database performance, reduced storage costs, and better type safety
story.tasks: - Task 1: Prisma Schema Conversion (AC: 1) - Task 2: TypeScript Enum Updates (AC: 2) - Task 3: Database Migration (AC: 3) - Task 4: Service Layer Updates (AC: 4) - Task 5: Testing (AC: 5) - Task 6: Performance Validation (AC: 6) - Task 7: Documentation & Deployment (AC: 7)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
1. [Prisma Schema Updates] All enum values in Prisma schema files converted from strings to integers - [ ] DomainStatus: ACTIVE=0, PASSIVE=1 - [ ] SmsType: OTP=0, NOTIFICATION=1, MARKETING=2, ALERT=3 - [ ] SmsStatus: PENDING=0, SENT=1, DELIVERED=2, FAILED=3 - [ ] Platform: iOS=0, Android=1
2. [TypeScript Enum Updates] All TypeScript enum files converted to numeric enums - [ ] ActionEnum: 13 values mapped to 0-12 - [ ] NotificationType: 6 values mapped to 0-5 - [ ] NotificationChannel: 3 values mapped to 0-2 - [ ] Platform enum updated
3. [Database Migration] Safe migration script created and applied - [ ] Backup existing data before conversion - [ ] Convert enum columns from string to integer type - [ ] Verify data integrity after migration - [ ] Test rollback procedure
4. [Code Updates] Service layer and DTOs updated to work with integer enums - [ ] Update DTO validation (@IsEnum decorators) - [ ] Remove manual string-to-enum conversions - [ ] Verify Prisma Client handles integer enums correctly - [ ] Update API endpoints to accept integer values
5. [Testing] Comprehensive test suite passes - [ ] Unit tests for all enum files (100% coverage) - [ ] DTO validation tests with integer enums - [ ] Integration tests verify data conversion - [ ] API endpoint tests with integer values - [ ] Full regression test suite passes
6. [Performance Validation] Performance improvements measured - [ ] Database query performance improved on enum columns - [ ] Storage reduction validated (~60% on enum columns) - [ ] API response size reduced for enum fields - [ ] No regression in application performance

doc.title: Technical Specification - Enum Integerization
doc.snippet: Convert all string-based enums to integer-based enums with proper mapping. Prisma Schema Updates, TypeScript Enum Conversion, Migration Strategy, Code Refactoring.

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Minimum Coverage: 70%. Utilities: 100%, Services: 80%+, Controllers: 70%+. Pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert for unit tests.

doc.title: Epic 14 Story 1 Context
doc.snippet: Previous story in Epic 14. Provides context for domain entity management and related enum usage.

file.reason: 13 values (CREATE, VIEW, UPDATE, etc.) to be converted from string to integer enum (0-12)
6 values to be converted to integer enum (0-5)
3 values to be converted to integer enum (0-2)
iOS, Android values to be converted to 0, 1
4 values (OTP, NOTIFICATION, MARKETING, ALERT) to be converted to 0-3
4 values (PENDING, SENT, DELIVERED, FAILED) to be converted to 0-3
DomainStatus enum: ACTIVE, PASSIVE values to be converted to 0, 1
Prisma schema enums to be converted from string to integer values
Line 580: type: type as string needs update to type: type as number for integer enum support
Verify Prisma enum conversion handles automatic integer handling

story-context.constraints: - Prisma Schema: Multi-file schema structure in prisma/schema/, enums defined with explicit integer values - TypeScript Enum Pattern: Kebab-case directory names (/enums/), UPPER_CASE enum values, single quotes - DTO Validation: @IsEnum decorator works with numeric enums, class-validator accepts integer values - Migration Safety: Backup required before migration, use CASE statements for string-to-int conversion - Testing Coverage: Minimum 70% overall, enum files require 100% coverage - Code Style: All files use single quotes, kebab-case file names, comprehensive JSDoc comments

interface.name: SmsType
interface.signature: enum SmsType { OTP = 0, NOTIFICATION = 1, MARKETING = 2, ALERT = 3 }

interface.name: SmsStatus
interface.signature: enum SmsStatus { PENDING = 0, SENT = 1, DELIVERED = 2, FAILED = 3 }

interface.name: ActionEnum
interface.signature: enum ActionEnum { CREATE = 0, VIEW = 1, UPDATE = 2, DELETE = 3, ASSIGN = 4, REVOKE = 5, ACTIVATE = 6, VIEW_ALL = 7, MARK_READ = 8, SEND = 9, MANAGE = 10, REGISTER = 11, VERIFY = 12, RESEND = 13 }

interface.name: NotificationType
interface.signature: enum NotificationType { VERIFICATION = 0, PASSWORD_RESET = 1, OTP = 2, GENERAL = 3, ALERT = 4, MARKETING = 5 }

tests.standards: Jest 30.0.0 framework with Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Unit tests for enum definitions verify integer values. Integration tests for DTO validation with integer enums. Migration tests verify data conversion integrity. Minimum 70% coverage overall, 100% on enum files. Use actual enum values in tests - no mocks needed for simple enum tests.
tests.locations: Unit tests in __tests__ directories alongside enum files. Integration tests in test/ directory. API tests use supertest. Test files follow *.spec.ts naming pattern. Mock factories available in test/factories/.

test.description: Unit tests for all enum files verify correct integer values (0, 1, 2, etc.)
test.example: describe('SmsType Enum', () => { it('should have correct integer values', () => { expect(SmsType.OTP).toBe(0); }); });

test.description: DTO validation tests accept valid integer enum values (0, 1, 2, 3)
Integration tests verify database operations work with integer enums
test.example: const sms = await prisma.sms.create({ data: { type: 0, status: 1 } }); expect(sms.type).toBe(0);

test.description: Migration tests verify data conversion accuracy from strings to integers
test.example: const domains = await prisma.domain.findMany(); domains.forEach(d => expect([0,1]).toContain(d.status));
